paper_title: Investigation of Human Activity and Environmental Impact at the CXGS Site

methods.description: Process for excavating a trench at the CXGS site and sampling sediment layers to investigate human activity and environmental impact.

steps.description: A 1.5 m × 0.5 m trench was chosen according to its high concentration of stone artefacts.
steps.searchable_summary: select trench location based on artefact concentration

steps.description: A 103 cm depth profile was obtained for sampling.
The profile was divided into five layers, with layer 4 assigned three sublayers according to the texture of the sediment and the cultural remains buried.
steps.searchable_summary: divide profile into layers and sublayers

steps.description: Charcoal samples from different layers were sent to the Peking University Radiocarbon Laboratory for radiocarbon dating.
steps.searchable_summary: send charcoal samples for radiocarbon dating

methods.description: Procedure for sampling sediments at specific intervals for water sieving and environmental analysis to assess human activity intensity and environmental conditions.

steps.description: Sediments of the profile were sampled at 5 cm intervals, starting from 20 cm for water sieving.
steps.searchable_summary: sample sediments at 5 cm intervals for water sieving

steps.description: Bone fragments and stone artefacts that had sunk to the bottom were collected for statistical analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bone fragments and artefacts for analysis

steps.description: Another sequence taken at 2 cm intervals from the profile was sampled for environmental analysis-specifically, charcoal concentration.
steps.searchable_summary: sample profile at 2 cm intervals for charcoal concentration

methods.description: Method for analyzing charcoal concentration to infer the intensity of paleofire events and human activities.

steps.description: Charcoals used for concentration measurement were acquired via heavy liquid flotation and were assessed via sampling statistics to acquire the charcoal concentration index.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire charcoal concentration index via heavy liquid flotation

steps.description: Charcoals were floated in a zinc diiodide (ZnI2) liquid and separated.
steps.searchable_summary: separate charcoals using zinc diiodide flotation

steps.description: A lower limit of 20 µm for charcoal particle size was adopted during the calculation.
steps.searchable_summary: apply 20 µm lower limit for charcoal particle size
